rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUTHIE MAE BISHOP
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/03/2023. The following procedural issues need to be cleared two weeks prior to the hearing: (1) Need further information in detail regarding the trust FOB Donald. Proposed distribution merely sets forth "Pooled Special Needs Trust" for the beneficiary; however, Petition does not aver if said trust has been created, if a joint agreement has been executed, if so when and by whom that happened. The trustee of said pooled SNT needs to be identified as the recipient of Donald's distribution and the trustee must be noticed. If the trust has not been established yet, this estate does not appear to be in condition to close. (2) Petitioner separately filed a copy of Power of Attorney of Donald without elaborating its purpose. Petition needs to make relevant allegation. LRSF 14.35.C. (3) Need further allegation in detail regarding the Jamie Bishop Special Needs Trust. Assuming it is an existing trust, the trustee needs to be identified and noticed. Otherwise, a separate petition to establish a SNT for the beneficiary may need to be filed in a proper court. (4) Need allegation why the cash distribution to the minor heir should not be deposited into blocked account until the minor reaches majority instead of deposit into UTMA account with custodian LRSF 14.35.E. (5) Need to show funds are being deposited in compliance with FDIC limit. Prob. Code 9700. (6) Need clarification if Donald still receives mails at the address listed as notice was sent to the real property sold in Nov. 2022. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY EVERETT MOATS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/26/2023, as the the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 05/23/2023) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No DE-120 form (including proof of service) is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. 3) No inventory and appraisal is on file. 4) The financial account statement verifying the ending cash balance must be filed (see LRSF 14.34(A)) with a declaration re retention of the original statement required by LRSF 14.59(A). 5) The beginning date of the account must be the date of the decedent's death. LRSF 14.34. 6) The accounting format and each schedule must comply with Probate Code sections 1061-1063. 7) Allegations required by section 1064(a)(1)-(5) must be provided. 8) Has the petitioner delivered all the estate assets to the successor personal representatives? If so, a signed receipt must be filed before the petitioner's bond can be exonerated on this petition. The descriptions of the assets on the signed receipt must match those on the schedule of assets at the end of the account period. An appearance may be required on 09/26/2023 if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELEANOR JUDITH DAVIS BYPASS TRUST CREATED UNDER THE DAVIS FAMILY REVOCABLE INTER VIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Account And Report Of Successor Trustee And Petition For Its Settlement And For Approval Of C
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar, as the following issues remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 04/05/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) Is Jeffrey McHenry represented by an attorney in the probate proceeding in Santa Clara County? If so, notice must also be given to his attorney. 3) Please provide a copy of a court order that appointed the petitioner as successor trustee of the subject sub-trust. If the petitioner was not appointed by any court, please state the date of acceptance of the sub-trust and explain how the appointment was effected. 4) The beginning balance of this account cannot be verified without settlement of the first account and its ending balance. Is the petitioner requesting an order merely acknowledging the account as presented? 5) Please explain why this sub-trust received distributions from the survivor's trust in April 2021. 6) Please explain why this tub-trust made distributions to the survivor's trust between January 2020 and April 2021. 7) Please explain why no distributions to the income beneficiary were reported during this account period. 8) Did the trustee exercise its discretion under section 6.4(b) of the governing trust by withholding distribution of any part of net income as unnecessary for the reasonable care, maintenance, support and education of the income beneficiary? If so, please state the total amount of undistributed net income and provide facts supporting the petitioner's necessity determination. 9) Without settlement of the first account and confirmation that the petitioner made all distribution of net income determined to be necessary for the income beneficiary during the first account period, the residue distributable to the remainder beneficiary as of the end of this account period cannot be ascertained. This matter will be called with PTR-23-306106. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELEANOR JUDITH DAVIS SURVIVOR'S TRUST CREATED UNDER THE DAVIS FAMILY REVOCABLE INTER VIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Fourth Account And Report Of Successor Trustee And Petition For Its Settlement; And Petition For App
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This matter will be called with PTR-22-305895. The following issues/tentative rulings remain unaddressed since the initial hearing on 05/16/2023: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) Is Jeffrey McHenry represented by an attorney in the probate proceeding in Santa Clara County? If so, notice must also be given to his attorney. 3) The beginning balance of this account cannot be verified without settlement of the prior account and its ending balance. Is the petitioner requesting an order merely acknowledging the account as presented? 4) Did the trustee exercise its discretion under section 6.1(d)(2) of the governing trust by withholding distribution of any part of net income as unnecessary for the reasonable care, maintenance, support and education of the income beneficiary? If so, please state the total amount of undistributed net income and provide facts supporting the petitioner's necessity determination. 5) Without settlement of the prior account and confirmation that the petitioner made all distribution of net income determined to be necessary for the income beneficiary during the prior account period, the residue distributable to the remainder beneficiary as of the end of this account period cannot be ascertained. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.
